title: Bounded Promise Workers, Origin Fairness, Failure Isolation, and Drain · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for bounded promise workers, origin fairness, failure isolation, and drain and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for origin-aware bounded async ingestion pool. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Derive global, origin, tenant, database, memory, queue, and event-loop budgets and enforce the tightest active limit.
Track queued, running, settling, canceled, failed, and completed jobs with exactly one owner and one terminal result.
Schedule per-origin work without monopolization while respecting per-publisher cadence and useful global throughput.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
